These are exactly the constructive-interference maxima allowed in a spherical cavity whose photon field is constrained to maximal 8-dimensional symmetry. The Gosset polytope formed by these directions is the unique stable resonance shell. Photon energy localises at the 240 interference nodes, forming the mass shell of the Planck star. PUH-BrianMartell Theorem 109 (Gosset Polytope as Photon Cavity Angular Mode Configuration — 240 Directions as Energy-Minimising Standing Waves in a Planck-Scale Cavity in PUH v64) In a Planck-scale spherical cavity the photon field forms standing-wave modes whose angular directions are labelled by spherical harmonics. Energy minimisation in 8-dimensional space selects the unique configuration with 240 directions: the Gosset polytope (4₂₁ polytope) whose vertices are precisely the E₈ root vectors. The 240 E₈ directions are therefore the natural angular mode skeleton of a resonant Planck cavity. Localisation at these vertices forms the mass shell. The Gosset polytope is the geometric attractor of the photon field, explaining why exactly 240 directions appear in the E₈ substrate.
